t1: Mini-STRC (700-1775, first pass) retains 5 of 14 potential sites; Ultra-Mini (1075-1775, clinical) retains only 2 high-confidence sites (N1179, N1274). NetNGlyc 1.0 (DTU Health Tech) uses neural networks trained on known glycoproteins to score each Asn-Xaa-Ser/Thr sequon (scores above 0.5 = predicted glycosylated; jury agreement x/9 networks indicates confidence). Reducing glycosylation density is a genuine engineering risk for ER quality control and tectorial-membrane anchoring, but both Ultra-Mini-retained sites are high-confidence and fall in the C-terminal ARM repeats where TMEM145 binding occurs. Each row below shows whether the site is retained in Mini-STRC and in Ultra-Mini.
t3: Result: Mini-STRC retains 5 of 14 sites (all confidently predicted, scores 0.52-0.72, jury 6-9/9); Ultra-Mini cuts this further to 2 high-confidence sites (N1179 at 0.678, N1274 at 0.517). The IgK signal peptide doesn't affect glycosylation scores (within 0.003 of no-SP control). N824 (score 0.452, below threshold) is retained in Mini but lost in Ultra; it's likely not glycosylated anyway. N696 sits in the disorder zone with a PRO-X1 warning and is a poor substrate even in the full protein.
t10: NetNGlyc 1.0 (Gupta & Brunak 2002). Job ID: 69BB95040023E1E80CE6DABF. All 3 constructs submitted as FASTA.
t23: 14. N-glycosylation site prediction (Mini-STRC vs Ultra-Mini) NetNGlyc 1.0
